text: This work deals with a relatively new type of hobby known as Urban Exploration (URBEX). URBEX is directly linked with the exploration of derelict and unused land and structures, brownfields. This documentation of modern ruins is carried out purely privately for photographic and videographic documentation and for sharing within a community or public exhibition. The properties visited in this way are however not owned by explorers and damage to the property or an endangering of their health and safety may therefore occur. It is therefore important to determine which part of the law applies to this relationship between these parties. The purpose of this work is to clarify, based on the Czech legislation, the rights and obligations of the property owner in the case that URBEX is carried out on his land.
